inline_comments.message:
We've changed the process for contributing new requirements a bit, and documented.  https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/VNFRQTS+How+to+Contribute#VNFRQTSHowtoContribute-SubmittingGerritReviews 

Couple things:
- You can generate unique requirement IDs using make_ids.py
- You need to generate the requirements using tox -e docs.  This will generate both the HTML version for your inspection, but also a JSON version of the requirements that will also be checked in and hosted on ReadTheDocs.
Is there a specific version of the spec that should be targeted?  Does it matter?

- request ID is generated (R-75943)
- 'tox -d docs' gives me error: "sphinx-build: error: config directory doesn't contain a conf.py file"
3GPP adds new features by releases. But it is always backward compatibility. Therefore it does not matter.

Yes, I tested this out, and there is an issue in the tox.ini file.

If you add -c . to the two lines below in tox.ini it should work

commands =
    sphinx-build -c . -b needs -n -d {envtmpdir}/doctrees ./docs/ ./docs/data/
    sphinx-build -c . -b html -n -d {envtmpdir}/doctrees ./docs/ {toxinidir}/docs/_build/html
